---
title: "SEM — model execution and path coefficient extraction"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    code-tools: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

## Overview

### Role in the pipeline

This script is the **third step** of the SEM pipeline, following:

1.  `data_preparation.qmd` — builds the full SEM database
2.  `sem_diagnostic.qmd` — selects the optimal window, completeness threshold, and transformations

Here, those validated parameters are applied to run the **full rolling-window pSEM** across all taxa and positions, and to generate a **null distribution via permutation** for inferential comparison.

### What this script produces

| Output | File | Description |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
| Observed path coefficients | `SEM_results_database.csv` | Standardised estimates, p-values, R², season labels for all taxon × position × window combinations |
| Null distribution summary | *(in memory)* | Mean and SD of path coefficients under random permutation of all causal variables |

### Statistical approach

For each rolling time window, two structural equations are fitted jointly as a piecewise SEM:

$$\text{Root} \leftarrow \beta_1 \cdot \text{microclimate}_1 + \beta_2 \cdot \text{microclimate}_2$$ $$\text{Fauna} \leftarrow \beta_3 \cdot \text{microclimate}_1 + \beta_4 \cdot \text{microclimate}_2 + \beta_5 \cdot \text{Root}$$

Each equation uses a **nested random intercept** (`orientation / depth`) and an **AR(1) correlation structure** to account for temporal autocorrelation within scanner groups. All variables are z-score standardised so that path coefficients (β) are directly comparable across predictors and taxa.

**Null distribution**: all causal variables (fauna, root, microclimate indices) are permuted independently across 30 iterations, breaking every directed link while preserving marginal distributions. Observed coefficients that lie far from the null mean (relative to the null SD) can be considered non-random.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Setup

```{r setup}
#| message: false

# ── Locale ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))

# ── Libraries ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
libs <- c(
  "tidyverse",    # Data wrangling and visualisation
  "lubridate",    # Date/time manipulation
  "piecewiseSEM", # Piecewise structural equation modelling
  "performance",  # Model performance metrics
  "lme4",         # Linear mixed-effects models
  "purrr",        # Functional iteration
  "nlme",         # LME with correlation structures (corAR1)
  "moments",      # Skewness and kurtosis
  "car",          # VIF
  "jsonlite",     # Reading the JSON parameter file
  "knitr"         # Table rendering
)
invisible(lapply(libs, require, character.only = TRUE))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Parameter loading

Parameters were determined in `sem_diagnostic.qmd` and saved to `model_parameters.txt`. If the file is missing, safe fallback defaults are used with a warning.

```{r load-params}
if (file.exists("../output/model_parameters.txt")) {

  best_params   <- read_json("../output/model_parameters.txt")
  WIN_DAYS      <- as.numeric(best_params$selected_window)
  MIN_RATE      <- as.numeric(best_params$selected_min_rate)
  TRANSFO_ROOT  <- as.character(best_params$transform_root)
  TRANSFO_FAUNA <- as.character(best_params$transform_fauna)

} else {
  # Fallback: safe defaults used when the config file is unavailable
  WIN_DAYS      <- 14
  MIN_RATE      <- 0.3
  TRANSFO_ROOT  <- "sqrt_plus_one"
  TRANSFO_FAUNA <- "sqrt_plus_one"
  warning("Configuration file not found. Using default fallback parameters.")
}

message(sprintf(
  "Active config — Window: %d days | Min. rate: %.1f | Transfo root: %s | Transfo fauna: %s",
  WIN_DAYS, MIN_RATE, TRANSFO_ROOT, TRANSFO_FAUNA
))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Data loading and preprocessing

```{r data-preparation}

# ── Load raw database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
data_sem_raw <- read.csv("../output/SEM_database.csv",
                         sep        = ",",
                         na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(
    position = as.factor(position),
    depth    = as.factor(depth),
    date     = ymd_hms(date, tz = "UTC"),
    root     = root_growth_cm2_count_24h   # 24-hour root growth flux (cm²)
  )

# ── Load metadata ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Taxa names and microclimate index column names, exported by data_preparation.qmd
fauna_vars         <- c("all_taxa", readLines("../output/fauna_vars.txt"))
microclimate_index <- readLines("../output/microclimate_index.txt")

# ── Transformation helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#' Apply a named variance-stabilising transformation to a numeric vector
#'
#' @param x       Numeric vector (counts or densities).
#' @param method  Character. One of: "none", "sqrt_plus_one", "log",
#'                "hellinger", "box_cox".
#' @return Transformed numeric vector.
apply_transfo <- function(x, method) {
  switch(method,
    "sqrt_plus_one" = sqrt(x) + sqrt(x + 1),           # Freeman-Tukey
    "log"           = log1p(x),                          # log(x + 1)
    "hellinger"     = sqrt(x / sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)),   # window-relative scale
    "box_cox"       = ((x + 1)^0.5 - 1) / 0.5,         # lambda = 0.5
    x                                                    # "none" or NA: identity
  )
}

# ── Apply differential transformations and standardise ────────────────────────
# Root and fauna variables may use different transformations (selected independently
# during the sensitivity analysis). All numeric variables are then z-score
# standardised so that path coefficients are directly comparable across taxa.
data_transformed <- data_sem_raw %>%
  mutate(root = apply_transfo(root, TRANSFO_ROOT)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    all_of(intersect(fauna_vars, names(.))),
    ~ apply_transfo(., TRANSFO_FAUNA)
  )) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Analytical functions

### 4.1 Rolling window splitter

```{r rolling-window-generator}
#' Split a time series into overlapping rolling windows
#'
#' @param df          Dataframe with a `date` column (POSIXct).
#' @param window_days Integer. Window duration in days.
#' @param step_days   Integer. Step between consecutive window start dates.
#' @return Named list of dataframes, one per window start date.
create_rolling_windows <- function(df, window_days, step_days = 1) {
  start_dates <- seq(
    min(df$date),
    max(df$date) - days(window_days) + hours(6),
    by = paste(step_days, "days")
  )

  windows        <- map(start_dates, ~ filter(df, date >= .x & date < .x + days(window_days)))
  names(windows) <- format(start_dates, "%Y-%m-%d")
  return(windows)
}
```

### 4.2 SEM structure

The same causal structure is applied to every rolling window, taxon, and position. Only the data change; the equations are fixed.

```{r SEM-structure}
#' Fit the piecewise SEM for a single data window
#'
#' @param data      Dataframe with columns: `root`, `fauna`, `microclimate_1`,
#'                  `microclimate_2`, `orientation`, `depth`.
#' @param m_indices Character vector of length 2 (microclimate index names).
#'                  Used by the pipeline wrapper; internally mapped to
#'                  `microclimate_1` and `microclimate_2`.
#' @return A `psem` object.
fit_psem <- function(data, m_indices) {
  psem(
    lme(root  ~ microclimate_1 + microclimate_2,
        data        = data,
        random      = ~ 1 | orientation / depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~ 1 | orientation / depth)),

    lme(fauna ~ microclimate_1 + microclimate_2 + root,
        data        = data,
        random      = ~ 1 | orientation / depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~ 1 | orientation / depth)),

    # Allow residual covariance between the two microclimate indices
    microclimate_1 %~~% microclimate_2
  )
}
```

### 4.3 Main pipeline

```{r fixed-pipeline}
#' Run the full rolling-window SEM pipeline for one taxon variable
#'
#' Iterates over all positions and time windows, fits the SEM, and extracts
#' standardised path coefficients and R² values.
#'
#' @param taxon_var   Character. Column name of the fauna variable to model.
#' @param data_raw    Dataframe. Transformed and z-score standardised SEM database.
#' @param m_indices   Character vector of length 2. Microclimate index column names.
#' @param window_days Integer. Window duration (days).
#' @param min_rate    Numeric [0, 1]. Minimum data completeness ratio.
#'                    Windows below this threshold are skipped.
#'                    (Max possible rows per window = window_days × 16:
#'                     4 time steps/day × 2 depths × 2 orientations.)
#' @return Dataframe with one row per path coefficient
#'         (taxon × position × window × predictor → response).
run_sem_pipeline <- function(taxon_var, data_raw, m_indices,
                             window_days, min_rate) {

  cat("\n--- Processing taxon:", taxon_var, "---\n")

  # Rename dynamic columns to fixed SEM internal names
  sem_data <- data_raw %>%
    mutate(
      fauna          = !!sym(taxon_var),
      microclimate_1 = !!sym(m_indices[1]),
      microclimate_2 = !!sym(m_indices[2])
    ) %>%
    select(date, position, orientation, depth,
           fauna, root, microclimate_1, microclimate_2)

  positions <- unique(sem_data$position)

  map_dfr(positions, function(pos) {

    df_pos  <- filter(sem_data, position == pos)
    windows <- create_rolling_windows(df_pos, window_days, step_days = 1)

    if (length(windows) == 0) return(NULL)

    # Discard windows with insufficient data density
    valid_wins <- keep(windows,
                       ~ (nrow(drop_na(.x)) / (window_days * 16)) >= min_rate)

    cat(sprintf("  Position %s: %d / %d windows passed filter.\n",
                pos, length(valid_wins), length(windows)))

    if (length(valid_wins) == 0) return(NULL)

    map_dfr(names(valid_wins), function(w_name) {
      win_data <- drop_na(valid_wins[[w_name]])

      # Guard: models will fail if a variable is constant (e.g., all-zero fauna)
      if (sd(win_data$fauna) == 0 || sd(win_data$root) == 0) return(NULL)

      # Fit the SEM; return NULL silently on convergence failure
      model <- tryCatch(fit_psem(win_data, m_indices), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(model)) return(NULL)

      # ── Extract path coefficients ──────────────────────────────────────────
      sm    <- summary(model)
      coefs <- as.data.frame(sm$coefficients)

      # ── Extract R² (marginal = fixed effects; conditional = fixed + random) ──
      # Column names vary across piecewiseSEM versions — detected dynamically
      r2_raw <- as.data.frame(sm$R2)
      r2_df  <- r2_raw %>%
        mutate(
          r2_cond = .[[grep("R.squared|Conditional", names(.))[1]]],
          r2_marg = .[[grep("Marginal",              names(.))[1]]]
        ) %>%
        select(response = Response, r2_cond, r2_marg)

      tibble(
        taxon        = taxon_var,
        position     = pos,
        date_start   = as.Date(w_name),
        n_obs        = sm$n,
        response     = coefs$Response,
        predictor    = coefs$Predictor,
        estimate     = coefs$Estimate,
        std_estimate = coefs$Std.Estimate,
        # "<0.001" strings are coerced to numeric by stripping the "<" prefix
        p_value      = as.numeric(str_replace(coefs$P.Value, "<", ""))
      ) %>%
        left_join(r2_df, by = "response")
    })
  })
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Model execution

```{r observed-models}
message(">>> Running observed models across all taxa...")

sem_results_raw <- map_dfr(
  fauna_vars,
  ~ run_sem_pipeline(.x, data_transformed, microclimate_index, WIN_DAYS, MIN_RATE)
)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Consolidation and export

```{r consolidation}

# ── Merge observed results with null summary ───────────────────────────────────
sem_results <- sem_results_raw %>%
  mutate(type = "observed") %>%
  # Keep only directed causal paths (→); remove correlated error terms (~~)
  filter(!str_detect(predictor, "~~")) %>%
  mutate(path_id = paste(predictor, "->", response))

# ── Season labels ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Assign meteorological seasons based on the window start month
season_lookup <- tibble(
  month  = 1:12,
  season = c("Winter", "Winter",
             "Spring", "Spring", "Spring",
             "Summer", "Summer", "Summer",
             "Autumn", "Autumn", "Autumn",
             "Winter")
)

sem_results <- sem_results %>%
  mutate(month = month(date_start)) %>%
  left_join(season_lookup, by = "month")

# ── Export ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
write.csv(sem_results, "../output/SEM_results_database.csv", row.names = FALSE)

message(sprintf(
  "Export complete: %d rows | %d unique taxa | %d unique paths",
  nrow(sem_results),
  n_distinct(sem_results$taxon),
  n_distinct(sem_results$path_id)
))
```
